name: game-options
description: How to read and change in-game settings (options.txt) via instance tools, including performance-relevant keys like render distance and VSync. Use when the user asks to check or change game settings, or wants tuning tips for render distance/graphics ("渲染距离","垂直同步","画质","游戏设置","选项").
body:
# Read/write game options

Tool calls: `instance(action=get_options, instance)` reads `options.txt`;
`instance(action=set_option, instance, key, value)` writes a single key.

## Common perf-relevant keys
- Render distance: 8–12 is a good range, not 32 — the far end costs heavy FPS.
- Fancy graphics: off trades looks for FPS.
- VSync: off if the user cares about input lag over screen tearing.

`get_options` isn't only for changing things — use it to just report the user's current
settings when asked (e.g. "what's my render distance") without touching anything.

## Don't
- Don't set render distance to the max "for best quality" without a one-line FPS-cost warning.
- Don't guess a key name — call `get_options` first to see what's actually in the file
  before `set_option`.
- Don't conflate this with `instance(action=set_memory)` — `options.txt` is in-game
  settings, not JVM heap size.
